title: Quantum Calorimetry
author_string: Stahle, Caroline Kilbourne, McCammon, Dan, Irwin, Kent D.
abstract: Your opponent's serve was almost perfect, but you vigorously returned it beyond his outstretched racquet to win the point. Now the tennis ball sits wedged in the chain-link fence around the court. What happened to the ball's kinetic energy? It has gone to heat the fence, of course, and you realize that if the fence were quite a bit colder, you might be able to measure that heat and determine just how energetic your swing really was.
